title: papaver pyrenaicum subsp. corona-sancti-stephani
intro: Papaver pyrenaicum subsp. corona-sancti-stephani, commonly known as the Spanish poppy or Crown of Saint Stephen, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Papaver pyrenaicum subsp. corona-sancti-stephani include relief from pain, anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate its medicinal properties.
